qas.q: in order to transport hazardous material which require placards
qas.a:
Must have CDL.
Must pass written test on hazardous material.
Must have Hazmat endorsement.

who needs to check that the shipper correctly named and labeled and marked hazmat

qas.a: Bulk container permanently attached to a vehicle

Hazardous Materials that don't allow you to park within 5 feet of road?

qas.a: Stop at safe place and repair

qas.q: When fueling a placard vehicle someone must always be?
qas.a: At the nozzle controlling the flow

qas.q: what type of extinguisher is required in the power unit of a placard vehicle.
qas.a: A U.I rating of 10 B/c or above

qas.a: send someone for help and warn others

qas.q: A placard on the trailer is intended to?
How many identical placards is a vehicle hauling hazmat required to display and where?
qas.a: four. one on front and back and one on each side

qas.q: The basic shipment of a hazmat product must include?
qas.a: proper shipping name, hazmat class and id number

qas.q: if X or RQ is in the HM column of a shipping paper entry the?
qas.a: shipment is regulated by hazmat regulations

qas.q: What placard must be used with inhalation hazmat load?
Which of the statements bout Radioactive labels is true?
qas.a: Yellow III indicates the highest level of radioactivity

Describe hazmat first.
Hazmat highlighted.
Id materials by X or RQ in HM column.

qas.a: to what shipping mode the information on that line appears

qas.a: Tabbed differently and kept on top of all other paperwork

qas.a:
In boxes that will keep them from turning over.
Loaded into racks attached to vehicle.
Loaded in an upright or horizontal position.

qas.q: if load requires placard what must shipper put on package?
qas.a:
The required label.
The hazmat shipping name and id number.
Name and address of shipper or consignee.

qas.q: it is acceptable to use placard with DANGEROUS if?
qas.a:
you load less then 5000 pounds or specified materials at one place.
You have two or more specified materials which total more than 1000 ponds.

qas.q: responsible for packaging, labeling and preparing hazmat shipping paper is?

qas.a: The person in attendance must be within 50 feet of the tanker.

qas.a: You can't haul FORBIDDEN cargo and nothing

qas.a: A government approved place for parking vehicles loaded with explosives.

qas.a: The hazmat id number of product.

RQ identifies what?

A placard must be?
qas.a: 3 inches away from any other markings

qas.a: The material does not need a placard.

qas.a: Products that can't be loaded together in same trailer.

qas.q: Hazard class of all materials specifies what?
qas.a: The risks associated with the materials

qas.a: Secure the cylinders in boxes or racks or brace flat.

5 feet of road.
300 feet of a tunnel.
near a fire.

qas.a: close all manholes and valves and be sure they are free of leaks

product ID markings on a tank must be?

Putting label on package.
Putting label on tag attached to product.
Putting a label on product.

qas.q: When transporting hazmat waste you must?
qas.a:
Have the word WASTE before the name on bills.
Sign a uniform Hazmat waste manifest.
Carry a uniform hazmat waste manifest.

qas.q: When a substance in the materials table is written in italics it means?
qas.a: it's not the proper shipping name. Only those in regular type are proper.

qas.q: A vehicle placard for division 1.1, 1.2 or 1.3 explosives.
qas.a: May be left unattended in a safe haven

qas.a:
Flares or fusees.
Reflective triangles.
Signal fires.

qas.a: The transport index and the separation distances tables.

qas.a: Can safely pass the fire without stopping.

qas.q: An A/W in the first column of hazmat table means?
qas.a: This material is only restricted when shipped by air and or water

within reach at all times.
In pouch on drivers door.
